Rationale: "good question . so we have a garden where all the flowers have two properties : color ( green or yellow ) and shape ( straight or curved ) . we ' re told that 1 / 5 of the garden is green , so , since all the flowers must be either green or yellow , we know that 4 / 5 are yellow . we ' re also told there is an equal probability of straight or curved , 1 / 2 . we want to find out the probability of something being yellow and straight , pr ( yellow and straight ) . so if we recall , the probability of two unique events occurring simultaneously is the product of the two probabilities , pr ( a and b ) = p ( a ) * p ( b ) . so we multiply the two probabilities , pr ( yellow ) * pr ( straight ) = 4 / 5 * 1 / 2 = 2 / 5 , or c ."

The student's answer is correct.

Simplifying the proportion, we get:

$x = 720$

error.teacher_advice_ch: 4^a*5^b是十的倍數會和分母的10^n互相消去，因此不能直接將n當作5，應先求出175質因數分解後為七的幾次方，再來推導出a,b和n的值
error.teacher_advice_en: Since 4^a * 5^b is a multiple of ten, it will cancel out with 10^n in the denominator. Therefore, n cannot be directly set to 5. First, find the number of times seven appears in the prime factorization of 175, and then derive the values of a, b, and n.

Problem: an army ’ s recruitment process included n rounds of selection tasks . for the first a rounds , the rejection percentage was 60 percent per round . for the next b rounds , the rejection percentage was 50 percent per round and for the remaining rounds , the selection percentage was 70 percent per round . if there were 100000 people who applied for the army and 175 were finally selected , what was the value of n ?
Rationale: "fastly i reduce 60 % till it gets closer to our required 2000 candidates step ( 1 ) 40000 accepted . step ( 2 ) another 40 % of 40000 = 16000 accepted . here it is quiet observable that if we further deduct candidate by 60 % it would change our probablity of easy going 2000 candidate . so i would get to second stage of recruitment where 50 % is accepted step ( 3 ) 50 % of 16000 = 8000 step ( 4 ) 50 % of 8000 = 4000 step ( 5 ) 50 % of 4000 = 2000 step ( 6 ) 50 % of 2000 = 1000 step ( 7 ) 50 % of 1000 = 500 step ( 8 ) 50 % of 500 = 250 step ( 9 ) 70 % of 350 = 175 ( our target ) total 9 steps required . ans b"

When the dice number is 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, the values of 3^s are 9, 81, 6261, 729, 9, 81, respectively. Therefore, the probability of 9 and 81 appearing is 2/6, while the probability of other values is 1/6. By calculating the probability of each solution found by the student and summing the probabilities, the correct answer can be obtained. Problem: the “ s - number ” of a number x is defined as the ones digit of 2 ^ x . antony rolls a die with 6 sides labeled with the integers from 1 to 6 , each of which has an equal probability of landing face - up . he then takes 3 ^ s , where s is the s - number of the result of his die roll , and plots 3 ^ s on a number line as the point a . finally , he repeats this entire process , this time plotting the result as the point b . what is the probability that the distance between a and b is greater than the value of b ?
Rationale: if you calculate 3 ^ s for 1 st roll , all 6 results will be 9 , 81 , 6561 , 729 , 9 , 81 . this result is the same for 2 nd roll . 9 , 81 , 6561 , 729 , 9 , 81 . about distance : if the first result is 9 and the second is also 9 , the distance is 9 - 9 = 0 which is smaller than 9 . if the first result is 9 and the second is 81 , the distance is 81 - 9 = 72 which is also smaller than b which has the value of 81 . if the first result is 81 and the second is 9 , the distance will be greater than b . distance 81 - 9 = 72 > 9 . on the first roll , the probability of getting result 9 is 2 / 6 . in this case no other alternative values for second roll which would make the distance greater than b . so probability is 0 . so next estimations are : probability of getting 81 on the first roll ( 2 / 6 ) * probability of getting 9 on the second roll ( 2 / 6 ) = 1 / 9 probability of getting 729 on the first roll ( 1 / 6 ) * probability of getting 9 , 81 on the second roll ( 4 / 6 ) = 1 / 9 probability of getting 6561 on the first roll ( 1 / 6 ) * probability of getting 9 , 81 , 729 on the first roll ( 5 / 6 ) = 5 / 36 all together : 1 / 9 + 1 / 9 + 5 / 36 = 13 / 36 = b

student_process: Assume the speed of danny is $x \mathrm{~m} /$ minute. $\Rightarrow$ the speed of steve is $\left(\frac{1}{2} x\right) \mathrm{m} /$ minute the distance between their house is $29 x$ $\Rightarrow$ the distance of the halfway point is $\left(\frac{24}{2}\right) x$ $$ \begin{aligned} & {\left[\left(\frac{29}{2} x\right) \div\left(\frac{1}{2} x\right)\right]-\left[\left(\frac{29}{2} x\right) \div x\right]} \\ & =29-\frac{29}{2} \\ & =\frac{29}{2}=14.5 \text { minutes } \end{aligned} $$
Problem: danny and steve are running towards each other , each one from his own house . danny can reach steve ' s house in 29 minutes of running , which is half the time it takes steve to reach danny ' s house . if the two started to run at the same time , how much time longer will it take steve to reach the halfway point between their houses than danny to reach the halfway point between their houses ?
Rationale: danny ' s time is 29 minutes . the time to reach halfway is 14.5 minutes . steve ' s time is 58 minutes . the time to reach halfway is 29 minutes . the time difference is 29 - 14.5 = 14.5 minutes the answer is a .
options: a ) 14.5 minutes , b ) 29 minutes , c ) 35 minutes , d ) 58 minutes , e ) 87 minutes

Problem: two stations p and q are 200 km apart on a straight track . one train starts from p at 7 a . m . and travels towards q at 20 kmph . another train starts from q at 8 a . m . and travels towards p at a speed of 25 kmph . at what time will they meet ?
Rationale: "assume both trains meet after x hours after 7 am distance covered by train starting from p in x hours = 20 x km distance covered by train starting from q in ( x - 1 ) hours = 25 ( x - 1 ) total distance = 200 = > 20 x + 25 ( x - 1 ) = 200 = > 45 x = 225 = > x = 5 means , they meet after 5 hours after 7 am , ie , they meet at 12 noon answer is b ."
options: a ) 10 am , b ) 12 noon , c ) 10.30 am , d ) 12.30 am , e ) 11 am

student_process: Assume there are $X$ passengers are on board the ship. $$ \begin{aligned} & \left(1-\frac{1}{12}-\frac{1}{8}-\frac{1}{3}-\frac{1}{6}\right) \cdot x=35 \\ & \Rightarrow\left(1-\frac{2}{24}-\frac{3}{24}-\frac{8}{24}-\frac{4}{24}\right) \cdot x=35 \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{7}{24} x=35 \\ & \Rightarrow x=35 \cdot \frac{24}{7}=120 \end{aligned} $$
Problem: if 1 / 12 of the passengers on a ship are from north america , 1 / 8 are europeans , 1 / 3 are from africa , 1 / 6 are from asia and the remaining 35 people are citizens of other continents , then how many passengers are on board the ship ?
Rationale: 1 / 12 + 1 / 8 + 1 / 3 + 1 / 6 = ( 2 + 3 + 8 + 4 ) / 24 = 17 / 24 let x be the number of passengers on the ship . 35 = ( 7 / 24 ) x x = 120 the answer is c .

Rationale: step 1 : find the cost of the ice cream . 19 × $ 7 = $ 133 step 2 : find the cost of the yoghurt . 4 × $ 1 = $ 4 step 3 : find how much more the ice cream cost than the yoghurt . $ 133 – $ 4 = $ 129 tara spent $ 129 more on ice cream . answer is e .

student_process: Assume the first tier's price level is $\$ X$ $$ \begin{aligned} & (x \cdot 12 \%)+(18000-x) \cdot 9 \%=1950 \\ & \Rightarrow 0,12 x+1620-0,09 x=1950 \\ & \Rightarrow 0,03 x=1950-1620=330 \\ & \Rightarrow x=330 \div 0,03=11000  \end{aligned} $$
Problem: country c imposes a two - tiered tax on imported cars : the first tier imposes a tax of 12 % of the car ' s price up to a certain price level . if the car ' s price is higher than the first tier ' s level , the tax on the portion of the price that exceeds this value is 9 % . if ron imported a $ 18,000 imported car and ended up paying $ 1950 in taxes , what is the first tier ' s price level ?
Rationale: let t be the tier price , p be total price = 18000 per the given conditions : 0.12 t + 0.09 ( p - t ) = 1950 0.12 t + 0.09 * 18000 - 0.09 t = 1950 0.03 t + 1620 = 1950 0.03 t = 1950 - 1620 = 330 t = 330 / 0.03 = 11000 answer b
annotated_formula: divide(subtract(1950, multiply(multiply(multiply(const_3, multiply(const_2, const_3)), const_1000), divide(9, const_100))), subtract(divide(12, const_100), divide(9, const_100)))

Problem: a dishonest dealer claims to sell a product at its cost price . he uses a counterfeit weight which is 20 % less than the real weight . further greed overtook him and he added 35 % impurities to the product . find the net profit percentage of the dealer ?
Rationale: "the dealer uses weight which is 20 % less than the real weight . or ( 1 - 1 / 5 ) or 4 / 5 of real weight . it means that he is selling $ 4 worth of product for $ 5 . the dealer then further added 35 % impurities to the product . it means that he is selling $ 5 worth of product for $ 6.75 . so his profit is $ 6.75 - $ 4 = $ 2 and his profit percent is ( 2.75 / 4 ) * 100 = 68.75 % answer : - c"

student_process: Assume there are $x$ hectares of the farm field \& the farmer planned $y$ days to work initially, $$ \begin{aligned} & \left\{\begin{array}{l} \frac{x}{160}=y \\ \frac{(x-40)}{85}=y+2 \end{array}\right. \\ & \Rightarrow\left\{\begin{array}{l} x=160 y-(1) \\ (x-40)=85(y+2)-(2) \end{array}\right. \end{aligned} $$ put $(1)$ into (2) $$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow(160 y-40)=85(y+2) \\ & \Rightarrow 160 y=85 y+170+40 \\ & \Rightarrow 75 y=210 \\ & \Rightarrow y=210 \div 75=\frac{42}{15}=\frac{14}{5}=2,8 \text { days } \\ & \text { put }(y=2.8) \text { into } 10 \\ & \Rightarrow x=160 \times 2,8=448 \text { hectares } \end{aligned} $$
Problem: if a farmer wants to plough a farm field on time , he must plough 160 hectares a day . for technical reasons he ploughed only 85 hectares a day , hence he had to plough 2 more days than he planned and he still has 40 hectares left . what is the area of the farm field and how many days the farmer planned to work initially ?
Rationale: "let x be the number of days in the initial plan . therefore , the whole field is 160 â ‹ … x hectares . the farmer had to work for x + 2 days , and he ploughed 85 ( x + 2 ) hectares , leaving 40 hectares unploughed . then we have the equation : 160 x = 85 ( x + 2 ) + 40 75 x = 210 x = 2.8 so the farmer planned to have the work done in 6 days , and the area of the farm field is 160 ( 2.8 ) = 448 hectares correct answer c"

llama_8b_wr:

The correct answer is (d) 32.

The student's approach is almost correct, but they missed the pattern of the ratio of the time taken for the nth round to that for the previous round. The pattern is to divide the time by 2 each time, so the correct answer is 32, which is 2^5, where 5 is the number of times to divide by 2.

The student is close, but they divided 7.5 by 8, which is not the correct approach. The correct approach is to divide the time by 2 each time, which would give the ratio 2, 4, 8, 16, ...

student_process: Assume the man can do $X$ perday, boy can do $y$ per day. $24(x+y) \Rightarrow$ total work load. $$ \begin{aligned} & 24(x+y)-18(x+y)=6(x+y) \Rightarrow \text { remaining work, } \\ & 6(x+y) \div x=26 \\ & \Rightarrow(x+y) \div x=\frac{13}{3} \\ & \Rightarrow 1+\frac{y}{x}=\frac{13}{3} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{y}{x}=\frac{10}{3} \\ & \Rightarrow 10 x=3 y \Rightarrow x=\frac{3 y}{10} \\ & 24(x+y) \div y \\ & =24\left(\frac{3 y}{10}+y\right) \div y \\ & =24 \cdot \frac{13}{10}=31.2 \text { days } \end{aligned} $$

Problem: a man and a boy complete a work together in 24 days . if for the last 6 days man alone does the work then it is completed in 26 days . how long the boy will take to complete the work alone ?
Rationale: explanation : ( man + boy ) ’ s 1 day ’ s work = 1 / 24 their 20 day ’ s work = 1 / 24 × 20 = 5 / 6 the remaining 1 / 6 work is done by the man in 6 days therefore , the man alone will finish the work in 6 × 6 days = 36 days man ’ s 1 day ’ s work = 1 / 36 therefore , boy ’ s 1 day ’ s work = 1 / 24 – 1 / 36 = 3 – 2 / 72 = 1 / 72 therefore , the boy alone will finish the work in 72 days . answer : option a
options: a ) 72 days , b ) 20 days , c ) 24 days , d ) 36 days , e ) 34 days

student_process: Assume she produce $X$ cabbages this year. $$ \begin{aligned} & \left\{\begin{array}{l} x=a^2-(1) \\ (x-191)=b^2-(2) \end{array}\right. \\ & \text { put (1) into (2) } \\ & \Rightarrow a^2-191=b^2 \\ & \Rightarrow(a+b)(a-b)=191 \\ & \Rightarrow(96+95)(96-95)=191 \\ & \Rightarrow\left\{\begin{array}{l} a=96 \\ b=95 \end{array}\right. \\ & x=96^2=9216  \end{aligned} $$
Problem: a gardener grows cabbages in her garden that is in the shape of a square . each cabbage takes 1 square feet of area in her garden . this year , she has increased her output by 191 cabbages as compared to last year . the shape of the area used for growing the cabbages has remained a square in both these years . how many cabbages did she produce this year ?
Rationale: "let the side for growing cabbages this year be x ft . thus the area is x ^ 2 . let the side for growing cabbages last year be y ft . thus , the area was y ^ 2 . the area would have increased by 191 sq ft as each cabbage takes 1 sq ft space . x ^ 2 - y ^ 2 = 191 ( x + y ) ( x - y ) = 191 191 is a prime number and thus it will be ( 96 + 95 ) * ( 96 - 95 ) . thus x = 96 and y = 95 x ^ 2 = 96 ^ 2 = 9216 the answer is c ."
options: a ) 7,251 , b ) 8406 , c ) 9216 , d ) 10,348 , e ) can not be determined

student_process: Assume the workload is $X$. a man can to $\frac{x}{6}$ per day. a boy can do $\frac{x}{18}$ per day. $$ \begin{aligned} & {\left[x-\left(\frac{x}{6}+\frac{x}{18}\right) \cdot 3\right] \div 3 } \\ & =\left(x-\frac{2 x}{3}\right) \div 3 \\ & =\frac{1}{3} x \cdot \frac{1}{3} \\ & =\frac{x}{9} \Rightarrow \text { a woman can do per day. } \\ \because & \frac{x}{9} \cdot 9=x \end{aligned} $$ $\therefore$ a woman need 9 days to finish alone.
Problem: a man , a woman and a boy can together complete a piece of work in 3 days . if a man alone can do it in 6 days and a boy alone in 18 days , how long will a woman take to complete the work ?

options: a ) 9 days , b ) 21 days , c ) 24 days , d ) 27 days , e ) 28 days

Problem: when tossed , a certain coin has an equal probability of landing on either side . if the coin is tossed 4 times , what is the probability that it will land on the same side each time ?
Rationale: on the first toss , the coin will land on one side or the other . on the second toss , the probability of landing on the same side is 1 / 2 . on the third toss , the probability of landing on the same side is 1 / 2 . on the fourth toss , the probability of landing on the same side is 1 / 2 . p ( same side all four times ) = 1 / 2 * 1 / 2 * 1 / 2 = 1 / 8 . the answer is c .
